enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Short Title and Commencement
1 These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Applicability
2 These Directions shall be applicable to All India Financial Institutions
(hereinafter collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as an 'AIFI'), viz.,
Export Import Bank of India ('EXIM Bank'), National Bank for Agriculture and
Rural Development ('NABARD'), Small Industries Development Bank of India
('SIDBI'), National Housing Bank ('NHB'), and National Bank for Financing
Infrastructure and Development ('NaBFID').
enriched_requirements.object: India Financial Institutions (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as an 'AIFI'), viz

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 3 These Directions shall become effective from the date of issue.
enriched_requirements.object: date of issue

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Definitions
4 In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein
shall bear the meaning as assigned to them below:
(1) 'Interest Rate Risk' is the risk where changes in market interest rates
might adversely affect an AIFI's financial condition.
enriched_requirements.object: assigned to them below: (1) 'Interest Rate Risk' is the risk where changes in market interest rates might adversely affect an AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Liquidity Risk' means inability of an AIFI to meet cash and collateral
obligations as they become due, without adversely affecting its financial
condition.
enriched_requirements.object: to meet cash and collateral obligations as they become due, without adversely affecting its financial condition

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) 'Stress Test' means evaluation of the financial position of an AIFI under a
severe but plausible scenario to assist in decision making within the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: position of an AIFI under a severe but plausible scenario to assist in decision making within the AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
5 All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the BR Act, the RBI Act, rules / regulations
made thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto or as
used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
enriched_requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the BR Act, the RBI Act, rules / regulations made thereunder, or any statutory modification

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Responsibilities of the Board
6 The Board shall have overall responsibility for management of risks and shall
decide the risk management policy of an AIFl and set limits for liquidity, interest
rate, exchange rate, and equity price risks.
enriched_requirements.object: management of risks and shall decide the risk management policy of an AIFl and set limits for liquidity, interest rate

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall constitute a
Management Committee or any other specific Committee, that shall oversee
the implementation of the Asset Liability Management (ALM) system and
review its functioning periodically.
enriched_requirements.object: or any other specific Committee, that shall oversee the implementation of the Asset Liability Management (ALM) syste

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Approval of policies, limits, and reviews
7 The Board shall formulate policy and strategy to manage liquidity risk, including
the liquidity risk tolerance.
enriched_requirements.object: to manage liquidity risk, including the liquidity risk tolerance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
8 The Board / Asset Liability Management Committee (ALCO) shall approve the
volume, composition, maximum maturity / duration, holding, and cut loss limits
for the Trading Book.
enriched_requirements.object: maximum maturity / duration, holding, and cut loss limits for the Trading Book

conditions:
Where the Board is satisfied that the AIFI has necessary
risk management capabilities
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where the Board is satisfied that the AIFI has necessary
risk management capabilities, it shall adopt an integrated Value at Risk (VaR)
limit for the entire balance sheet, including the "Banking Book" and the "Trading
Book", for the rupee as well as foreign currency portfolio.
enriched_requirements.object: at Risk (VaR) limit for the entire balance sheet, including the "Banking Book" and the "Trading Book", for the rupee as well

conditions: Where an AIFI has adequate MIS and technical capability
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
9 Where an AIFI has adequate MIS and technical capability, the Board / ALCO
shall ensure to move to the modern techniques of Interest Rate Risk
measurement like Duration Gap Analysis, Simulation, and Value at Risk
10 The Board / ALCO shall approve internal prudential limits for cumulative
negative liquidity gaps across all time buckets.
enriched_requirements.object: the modern techniques of Interest Rate Risk measurement like Duration Gap Analysis, Simulation, and Value at Risk 10 The Board / ALC

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
11 The Board / ALCO shall approve the prudential limits for interest rate gaps in
various time buckets.
enriched_requirements.object: for interest rate gaps in various time buckets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such limits on interest rate risk shall be set as simple gap
limits or in terms of impact on Net Interest Income (NII) / Net Interest Margin
(NIM) or Earnings at Risk (EaR) of the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: gap limits or in terms of impact on Net Interest Income (NII) / Net Interest Margin (NIM) or Earnings at Risk (EaR) of the AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
12 The Board / ALCO shall approve the classification of components of assets and
liabilities in the appropriate time buckets, through reasonable estimation of
behavioural patterns, embedded options, and rolls-in and rolls-out based on
past data or empirical studies.
enriched_requirements.object: components of assets and liabilities in the appropriate time buckets, through reasonable estimation of behavioural patte

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Introduction
13 An AIFI shall establish an ALM framework for measuring, monitoring, and
managing liquidity risk, interest rate risk, and foreign exchange risks.
enriched_requirements.object: for measuring, monitoring, and managing liquidity risk, interest rate risk, and foreign exchange risks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
system shall involve the identification and assessment of various types of risks
and enable dynamic adjustment of balance sheet items (assets and liabilities)
to manage these risks effectively.
enriched_requirements.object: assessment of various types of risks and enable dynamic adjustment of balance sheet items (assets and liabilities) to m

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
14 The ALM system shall introduce a formalised framework for management of
market risks through measuring, monitoring and managing liquidity, exchange
rate, and interest rate risks of an AIFI that need to be closely integrated with the
AIFI's business strategy.
enriched_requirements.object: for management of market risks through measuring, monitoring and managing liquidity, exchange rate, and interest rate

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALM function shall also endeavour to enforce the
discipline of market risk management, viz., managing business after assessing
the market risks involved.
enriched_requirements.object: the discipline of market risk management, viz

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
ALM Information System
16 The ALM framework shall be supported by risk policies and tolerance limits.
enriched_requirements.object: policies and tolerance limits

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Top Management shall ensure the implementation of necessary
information system and availability of timely, adequate, and accurate
information, which shall be central to the ALM process.
enriched_requirements.object: necessary information system and availability of timely, adequate, and accurate information, which shall be central to th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
17 An AIFI shall, at a minimum, establish systems sufficient to generate liquidity
gap and interest rate gap reports in accordance with these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: establish systems sufficient to generate liquidity gap and interest rate gap reports in accordance with these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
ALM Organisation
18 The Top Management of the AIFI shall be responsible for successful
implementation of the risk management framework.
enriched_requirements.object: implementation of the risk management framework

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The risk management
framework shall be integrated with the AIFI's basic operations and strategic
decision-making.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI's basic operations and strategic decision-making

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
19 An AIFI shall set up an ALCO as a decision-making unit responsible for
integrated balance sheet management from a risk-return perspective, including
the strategic management of interest rate and liquidity risks.
enriched_requirements.object: as a decision-making unit responsible for integrated balance sheet management from a risk-return perspective, including the strategic m

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While each AIFI
shall define the roles, responsibilities, and powers of its ALCO, its responsibility
would normally include:
(1) monitoring the market risk levels of the AIFl by ensuring adherence to the
various risk limits set by the Board;
enriched_requirements.object: and powers of its ALCO, its responsibility would normally include: (1) monitoring the market risk levels of the AI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Future business strategy shall be aligned with
the interest rate outlook as also on other parameters considered relevant;
enriched_requirements.object: interest rate outlook as also on other parameters considered relevant

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Composition of ALCO
20 The composition of ALCO in an AIFI shall depend on the size of the AIFI,
business mix and its organisational complexity.
enriched_requirements.object: of the AIFI, business mix and its organisational complexity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The CEO / CMD / DMD or the
ED should head the Committee.

The Top Management from various relevant
<image>
functions / departments shall be represented in the ALCO.
enriched_requirements.object: ALCO

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall
include, as its members, the Heads of Investment, Credit, Resources
Management, Treasury, Risk Management, and other members as deemed
suitable.
enriched_requirements.object: the Heads of Investment, Credit, Resources Management, Treasury, Risk Management, and other members as deemed suitable

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: An AIFI shall decide the frequency of ALCO meetings.
enriched_requirements.object: ALCO meetings

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
21 An AIFI may also constitute an ALM Support Group, consisting of the operating
staff, which shall be responsible for analysing, monitoring, and reporting the risk
profiles to the ALCO.
enriched_requirements.object: monitoring, and reporting the risk profiles to the ALCO

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The staff shall also prepare forecasts (simulations)
reflecting the impact of various possible changes in market conditions on the
balance sheet and recommend the action needed to adhere to internal limits.
enriched_requirements.object: reflecting the impact of various possible changes in market conditions on the balance sheet and recommend the act
Liquidity risk management

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Management of Liquidity Risk
23 An AIFI should assess the risks resulting from its inability to meet its obligations
as they come due, because of difficulty in liquidating assets (market liquidity
risk) or in obtaining adequate funding (funding liquidity risk).
enriched_requirements.object: from its inability to meet its obligations as they come due, because of difficulty in liquidating assets (market liquidity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This assessment
should include analysis of sources and uses of funds, an understanding of the
funding markets in which the AIFI operates, and an assessment of the efficacy
of a contingency funding plan for events that could arise.
enriched_requirements.object: and uses of funds, an understanding of the funding markets in which the AIFI operates, and an assessment of the efficacy o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Top Management
shall consider the relationship between liquidity and capital since liquidity risk
can impact capital adequacy which, in turn, can aggravate an AIFI's liquidity
risks.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity and capital since liquidity risk can impact capital adequacy which, in turn, can aggravate an AIFI's liqui

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The risk tolerance shall be communicated throughout the AIFI and be reflected
in the strategy and policies that Top Management set to manage liquidity risk.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI and be reflected in the strategy and policies that Top Management set to manage liquidity risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, an AIFI shall take into account the cost, benefit, and risk of liquidity into
its internal pricing, performance measurement, and new product approval
process of all significant business activities.
enriched_requirements.object: cost, benefit, and risk of liquidity into its internal pricing, performance measurement, and new product approval process of all

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
25 An AIFI shall ensure time bucketing of contingent cash flows emanating from
the off-balance sheet items as per the timing of the underlying cash flows for
the purpose of compiling the liquidity and interest rate gap reports, as
discussed in subsequent paragraphs.
enriched_requirements.object: contingent cash flows emanating from the off-balance sheet items as per the timing of the underlying cash flows for the purpo

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
26 An AIFI shall set up a robust framework to identify, measure, monitor, and
control liquidity risk.
enriched_requirements.object: framework to identify, measure, monitor, and control liquidity risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The framework shall project cash flows arising from assets,
liabilities, and off-balance sheet items over an appropriate set of time horizons.
enriched_requirements.object: from assets, liabilities, and off-balance sheet items over an appropriate set of time horizons

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The process shall also ensure diversification in both the tenor and source of
funding.
enriched_requirements.object: both the tenor and source of funding

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
27 An AIFI shall utilise early warning indicators to identify the emergence of
increased risk or vulnerabilities in its liquidity position or funding needs.
enriched_requirements.object: to identify the emergence of increased risk or vulnerabilities in its liquidity position or funding needs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It shall
have the ability to control liquidity risk exposure and funding needs, regardless
of its organisation structure, within and across legal entities, business lines, and
<image>
currencies, taking into account any legal, regulatory and operational limitations
to the transferability of liquidity.
enriched_requirements.object: control liquidity risk exposure and funding needs, regardless of its organisation structure, within and across legal entities, bus

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
28 An AIFI shall perform stress tests or scenario analyses on a regular basis in
order to identify and quantify its exposures to possible future liquidity stresses,
analysing possible impacts on the institutions' cash flows, liquidity positions,
profitability, and solvency.
enriched_requirements.object: scenario analyses on a regular basis in order to identify and quantify its exposures to possible future liquidity stresses, an

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The results of these stress tests shall be discussed
thoroughly by the Top Management.
enriched_requirements.object: the Top Management

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These discussions, shall form the basis for
taking remedial or mitigating actions to limit the AIFI's exposures, build up a
liquidity cushion, and adjust its liquidity profile to fit its risk tolerance.
enriched_requirements.object: taking remedial or mitigating actions to limit the AIFI's exposures, build up a liquidity cushion, and adjust its liquidity profile

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
results of stress tests shall also play a key role in shaping the AIFI's
contingency funding planning, which shall outline policies for managing a range
of stress events and clearly set out strategies for addressing liquidity shortfalls
in emergency situations.
enriched_requirements.object: role in shaping the AIFI's contingency funding planning, which shall outline policies for managing a range of stress events and clearl

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Statement of Liquidity
29 An AIFI shall adopt the maturity ladder approach as the standard tool for
measuring and managing net funding requirements through preparation of
Statement of Liquidity as furnished in Annex-I.
enriched_requirements.object: approach as the standard tool for measuring and managing net funding requirements through preparation of Statement of Liquid

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
30 The Maturity Profile, as detailed in Annex-III, shall be used for measuring the
future cash flows of AIFIs in different time buckets.
enriched_requirements.object: the future cash flows of AIFIs in different time buckets
(1) 1 to 14 days

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
31 The securities which are held in the Trading Book shall be subject to the
following preconditions:
<image>
(1) The composition and volume of the Trading Book shall be clearly defined;
enriched_requirements.object: following preconditions: <image> (1) The composition and volume of the Trading Book shall be clearly defined

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (2) Maximum maturity / duration of the trading portfolio shall be restricted;
(4) Cut-loss limit(s) shall be prescribed;

and
(5) Such securities shall be marked-to-market on a daily / weekly basis and
the revaluation gain / loss shall be charged to the profit and loss account.
enriched_requirements.object: daily / weekly basis and the revaluation gain / loss shall be charged to the profit and loss account

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
33 The Board / ALCO of the AIFI shall approve the volume, composition,
maximum maturity / duration, holding / defeasance period, and cut loss limits of
the Trading Book.
enriched_requirements.object: maximum maturity / duration, holding / defeasance period, and cut loss limits of the Trading Book

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
34 An AIFI that has the necessary risk management capabilities, shall have the
option, with the approval of its Board, to adopt an integrated Value at Risk
(VaR) limit for its entire balance sheet, including the "Banking Book" and the
"Trading Book", for the rupee as well as foreign currency portfolio.
enriched_requirements.object: the approval of its Board, to adopt an integrated Value at Risk (VaR) limit for its entire balance sheet, including the "Banking

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the
approved policy note in this regard, shall be submitted to the Department of
Supervision (DoS), Reserve Bank of India.
enriched_requirements.object: Department of Supervision (DoS), Reserve Bank of India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall also be guided by the
Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation
and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Direc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
35 Within each time bucket, an AIFI shall monitor mismatches arising from cash
inflows and outflows.
enriched_requirements.object: cash inflows and outflows

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The negative gap for 1-14 days and 15-28 days time-
buckets shall not exceed 10 per cent and 15 per cent respectively, of the cash
outflows in each time bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: and 15 per cent respectively, of the cash outflows in each time bucket

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall also monitor the cumulative
mismatches (running total) across all time buckets by establishing internal
prudential limits approved by the Board / ALCO.
enriched_requirements.object: mismatches (running total) across all time buckets by establishing internal prudential limits approved by the Board / ALCO

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 36 An AIFI shall prepare the Statement of Liquidity in the format prescribed at
enriched_requirements.object: Liquidity in the format prescribed at

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A maturing liability shall be a
cash outflow while a maturing asset will be a cash inflow.
enriched_requirements.object: while a maturing asset will be a cash inflow

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Chapter V - Management of Currency Risk
38 In order to capture the Liquidity and Interest Rate Risk arising out of the foreign
currency portfolio, an AIFI shall prepare, on an ongoing basis, currency-wise
Statement of Liquidity and Interest Rate Sensitivity (IRS) Statement, separately
for each of the currencies in which it has an exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: basis, currency-wise Statement of Liquidity and Interest Rate Sensitivity (IRS) Statement, separately for each of the currencie

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These statements shall
be prepared in the formats prescribed at Annex I-A and Annex-II-A, which are
similar to the formats prescribed for the rupee resources, at Annex-I and
enriched_requirements.object: formats prescribed at Annex I-A and Annex-II-A, which are similar to the formats prescribed for the rupee resources, at Annex-I and

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
40 An AIFI shall use the Traditional Gap Analysis (TGA) to measure the interest
rate risk.
enriched_requirements.object: Analysis (TGA) to measure the interest rate risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Traditional Gap Analysis
41 Under TGA, an AIFI shall measure Gap or Mismatch risk by calculating Gaps,
i.e., difference between rate sensitive liabilities and rate sensitive assets
(including off-balance sheet positions), over different time intervals at a given
date.
enriched_requirements.object: risk by calculating Gaps, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
42 An AIFI shall prepare the Gap Report by grouping rate sensitive liabilities,
assets and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets according to residual
maturity or next re-pricing period, whichever is earlier.
enriched_requirements.object: by grouping rate sensitive liabilities, assets and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets according to residual maturity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All investments,
advances, deposits, borrowings, and purchased funds, that mature / re-price
within a specified timeframe, shall be treated as interest rate sensitive.
enriched_requirements.object: rate sensitive

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any
<image>
principal repayment of loan, including final principal payment and interim
instalment expected to be received within the time horizon shall also be
classified as rate sensitive.
enriched_requirements.object: rate sensitive

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Interest bearing assets and liabilities that vary with
a reference rate and reprice at pre-determined intervals shall be treated as rate
sensitive at the time of repricing.
enriched_requirements.object: sensitive at the time of repricing

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While the interest rates on term deposits are
fixed during their currency, the tranches of advances portfolio with floating rates
shall be treated as rate sensitive.
enriched_requirements.object: sensitive

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Interest Rate Sensitivity Statement
43 An AIFI shall prepare Interest Rate Sensitivity statement as per the reporting
format provided in Annex-II.
enriched_requirements.object: statement as per the reporting format provided in Annex-II

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
44 An AIFI shall identify the interest rate gaps in the following time buckets:
(1) 1-28 days;
enriched_requirements.object: gaps in the following time buckets: (1) 1-28 days

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
45 An AIFI shall slot the securities in the Trading Book as per their residual
maturity or repricing maturity for floating rate securities.
enriched_requirements.object: the Trading Book as per their residual maturity or repricing maturity for floating rate securities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The various items of
rate sensitive assets, liabilities and off-balance sheet items shall be classified
into respective time-buckets, as explained in Annex-IV.
enriched_requirements.object: time-buckets, as explained in Annex-IV

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
46 An AIFI shall set prudential limits on individual Gaps with the approval of the
Board / ALCO.
enriched_requirements.object: individual Gaps with the approval of the Board / ALCO

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The prudential limits shall have a relationship with the Total
Assets, Earning Assets, or Equity.
enriched_requirements.object: the Total Assets, Earning Assets, or Equity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the interest rate gap limits, an
AIFI which has adequate systems in place may set the prudential limits linked
to EaR or NIM based on its views on interest rate movements, with the
approval of the Board / ALCO.
enriched_requirements.object: linked to EaR or NIM based on its views on interest rate movements, with the approval of the Board / ALCO

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Behavioural Pattern and Embedded Options
47 The classification of various components of assets and liabilities into different
time buckets for preparation of Gap reports (Liquidity and Interest Rate
Sensitivity) may be as indicated in Annex-I and Annex-II.
enriched_requirements.object: Annex-I and Annex-II

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
48 However, an AIFI which is better equipped to reasonably estimate the
behavioural pattern, embedded options, rolls-in and rolls-out, etc., of various
components of assets and liabilities on the basis of past data / empirical studies
may choose to classify them in the appropriate time buckets, subject to the
approval from its ALCO / Board.
enriched_requirements.object: in the appropriate time buckets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the note approved by the ALCO /
Board shall be sent to the Department of Supervision (DoS), RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: Department of Supervision (DoS), RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
49 An AIFI shall assess and manage the risk arising from embedded options such
as premature closure of term deposits, early encashment of bonds, and
prepayment of loans and advances, which can significantly affect its liquidity
and interest rate risk profile, particularly during periods of market volatility.
enriched_requirements.object: risk arising from embedded options such as premature closure of term deposits, early encashment of bonds, and prepayment of loan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An
AIFI shall develop suitable mechanism, supported by empirical studies and
behavioural analysis, to estimate the future behaviour of assets, liabilities, and
off-balance sheet items to changes in market variables and estimate the impact
of embedded options.
enriched_requirements.object: by empirical studies and behavioural analysis, to estimate the future behaviour of assets, liabilities, and off-

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the absence of adequate historical database, an AIFI
shall classify the entire amount payable under the embedded options in the
maturity ladder according to the residual period up to the earliest exercise date.
enriched_requirements.object: payable under the embedded options in the maturity ladder according to the residual period up to the earliest exercise date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Internal Transfer Pricing
50 An AIFI shall implement a scientifically designed internal transfer pricing (ITP)
mechanism as part of its ALM system.
enriched_requirements.object: internal transfer pricing (ITP) mechanism as part of its ALM system

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The transfer pricing mechanism shall
enable centralisation of interest rate risk management, thereby facilitating
effective oversight and control, and also provide a rational and consistent
framework for the pricing of assets and liabilities.
enriched_requirements.object: rate risk management, thereby facilitating effective oversight and control, and also provide a rational and consiste

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: An AIFI shall prepare the Statement of Liquidity on a fortnightly basis, as per
enriched_requirements.object: Liquidity on a fortnightly basis, as per

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall prepare the Interest Rate Sensitivity statement on a monthly
basis, as per Chapter VI.
enriched_requirements.object: Sensitivity statement on a monthly basis, as per Chapter VI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Top Management / Board/ ALCO shall regularly review these statements and
formulate corrective measures and devise suitable strategies wherever needed.
enriched_requirements.object: and formulate corrective measures and devise suitable strategies wherever needed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall submit the Statement of Liquidity and the Interest Rate Sensitivity
Statement to Department of Supervision (DoS), RBI on a quarterly basis.
enriched_requirements.object: Liquidity and the Interest Rate Sensitivity Statement to Department of Supervision (DoS), RBI on a quarterly basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
Directions, instructions and guidelines already repealed shall continue to
remain repealed.

56 Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may
be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or
punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or
guidelines had not been repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Application of other laws not barred
57 The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or Directions, for the time
being in force.
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or Directions, for the time being in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Interpretations
58 For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of
any provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.

in millions)
Items/ Time buckets 1 to 14 days
29 days to 3 months
Over 6 months to 1 year
Over 1 year to 3

Over 3 to

10 years Over 10
years Total
years
Item-wise details may be furnished under each category in the respective time- buckets
enriched_requirements.object: category in the respective time- buckets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
INFLOWS

Items/ Time buckets 1 to 14 days
15 t0 28 days

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, if such gifts, grants, etc., are tied to specific end-use, then these may be slotted in the time- bucket as per purpose/end-use specified.
enriched_requirements.object: time- bucket as per purpose/end-use specified

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The amount of foreign currency bonds/ borrowings which has been deployed in foreign currency assets on a back-to-back basis, should be reported in the currency-wise liquidity statement for the relative currency.
enriched_requirements.object: currency-wise liquidity statement for the relative currency

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the absence of
<image>
adequate historical database, the entire amount payable under the embedded options should be slotted as per the residual period to the earliest exercise date.
enriched_requirements.object: the residual period to the earliest exercise date

conditions: if issued without any embedded options
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: c) Zero-coupon / deep discount bonds Such bonds, if issued without any embedded options, should be shown in the time bucket corresponding to their residual maturity for the full amount of maturity / face value.
enriched_requirements.object: time bucket corresponding to their residual maturity for the full amount of maturity / face value

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: b) Inter Corporate Deposits These being institutional / wholesale deposits, should be slotted as per their residual maturity.
enriched_requirements.object: their residual maturity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The cash outflows should be slotted in respective time buckets as per the residual period to the due date of payment.
enriched_requirements.object: time buckets as per the residual period to the due date of payment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The amount of interest overdue (including the amount pre- funded in the account with RBI for servicing of outstanding SLR bonds pending claims from investors) should be shown in 1 - 14 days time-bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: - 14 days time-bucket

conditions: In case of floating rate bonds/deposits
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In case of floating rate bonds/deposits, the amount of interest outflow may be calculated at the floating rate applicable on the reporting date.
enriched_requirements.object: floating rate applicable on the reporting date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: e) Provisions for NPAs and the Standard assets The amount of provision may be netted out from the gross amount of the loan portfolio and the net amount of loan assets be shown as an item under inflows in stipulated time-buckets.
enriched_requirements.object: the gross amount of the loan portfolio and the net amount of loan assets be shown as an item under inflows in stipulated time-bucke

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: f) Provision for Investments portfolio The amount may be netted from the gross value of investments portfolio and the net investments be shown as inflow in the prescribed time-slots.
enriched_requirements.object: gross value of investments portfolio and the net investments be shown as inflow in the prescribed time-slots

conditions: In case provisions are not held security- wise
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In case provisions are not held security- wise, the provision may be shown in 'over 10 years' bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: 10 years' bucket

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, the bonds/debentures valued by applying NPA norms due to non-servicing of interest, should be shown in time buckets as prescribed below at items 7(a) and 7(b) respectively.
enriched_requirements.object: buckets as prescribed below at items 7(a) and 7(b) respectively

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
package, should be slotted as per the specific disinvestment
plan formulated for such holdings.
enriched_requirements.object: the specific disinvestment plan formulated for such holdings

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In case, a specific disinvestment plan has not been evolved, the equity holdings should be shown as an inflow in the 'Over 10 year' bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: inflow in the 'Over 10 year' bucket

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
b) Term loans (rupee loans including the export Loans funded out of rupee resources but denominated in foreign currencies)
The cash inflows on account of the interest and principal of the loan may be slotted in respective time buckets as per the timing of the cash flows as stipulated in the original/revised repayment schedule.
enriched_requirements.object: time buckets as per the timing of the cash flows as stipulated in the original/revised repayment schedule

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The AIFI which finance its exporter-clients out of rupee resources but denominate such loans in their books in foreign currency to be extinguished out of the export proceeds, should report such assets in the rupee liquidity statement since such loans are funded out of rupee resources.
enriched_requirements.object: the rupee liquidity statement since such loans are funded out of rupee resources

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, the AIFI which have the adequate database on the past pre-payment behaviour of such loans, may undertake time- series analysis and slot such loans as per behavioural maturity.
enriched_requirements.object: and slot such loans as per behavioural maturity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Non-performing loans
(May be shown net of the provisions, interest suspense held and the amount of claims received from ECGC.)
a) Sub-standard
<image>
i) All overdues and instalments of principal falling due during the next three years
In the 3 to 5 year time-bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: the provisions, interest suspense held and the amount of claims received from ECGC

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Assets on lease Entire cash flows from the lease transaction, representing principal as well as interest element, may be slotted in respective time buckets as per the timing of the cash flow.
enriched_requirements.object: time buckets as per the timing of the cash flow

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: As regards the future income from assets, the cash inflow over the entire life of the asset should be captured and not only till the reporting date.
enriched_requirements.object: only till the reporting date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, for loan assets, the future income should be reckoned only in respect of standard assets at the level existing on the reporting date and for debt securities, the interest income should be reckoned as inflow only if the interest is serviced regularly.
enriched_requirements.object: respect of standard assets at the level existing on the reporting date and for debt securities, the interest income should be reck

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Contingent cash flows

a) Letters of credit/guarantees (outflow through Devolvement)
Based on the past trend analysis of the devolvements vis-a-vis the outstanding amount of LCs / guarantees (net of margins held), the likely devolvements should be estimated and this amount could be distributed in various time buckets on judgmental basis.
enriched_requirements.object: amount could be distributed in various time buckets on judgmental basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The assets created out of devolvements may be shown under respective maturity buckets on the basis of probable recovery dates.
enriched_requirements.object: maturity buckets on the basis of probable recovery dates

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The LCs established against sanctioned assistance would be in the nature of undisbursed commitments and should be slotted as an outflow in the time-buckets arrived at keeping in view the validity period of LC / shipment schedule under LC / due date of bills stipulated under the LC b) Loan commitments pending disbursal / undisbursed commitments (outflow)
Only that amount of undisbursed commitments should be captured in the liquidity report in respect of which the notice for draw- down has been received from the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: outflow in the time-buckets arrived at keeping in view the validity period of LC / shipment schedule under LC / due date of bills sti

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In case, however, the terms of sanction do not stipulate any notice to be given by the borrower, the entire amount of undisbursed commitment should be slotted in the respective time buckets as per the sanctioned disbursement schedule.
enriched_requirements.object: respective time buckets as per the sanctioned disbursement schedule

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
c) Repayments against the undisbursed commitments
Such future inflows should be slotted in the relative time buckets as per the stipulated repayment schedule under the terms of sanction, only in respect of the amount reckoned at item (b) above.
enriched_requirements.object: relative time buckets as per the stipulated repayment schedule under the terms of sanction, only in respect of the amount reckoned a

conditions: if the draw-down under the line of credit is subject to a specified notice period of more than 14 days
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, if the draw-down under the line of credit is subject to a specified notice period of more than 14 days, such notice period should also be reckoned in deciding the appropriate time bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: deciding the appropriate time bucket

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: e) Underwriting commitments (outflow) Based on the analysis of the past trend of devolvement of underwriting commitments, the amount of such commitments may be slotted in the relative time bucket as per the time schedule of IPO/finalisation of allotment.
enriched_requirements.object: relative time bucket as per the time schedule of IPO/finalisation of allotment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The cash flows relating to currency swaps may be shown in the liquidity statement of the respective currencies.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity statement of the respective currencies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: outflow due to wage settlement arrears, capital expenses, income tax refunds, etc.) should be shown in a time bucket corresponding to timing of such cash flows.
enriched_requirements.object: time bucket corresponding to timing of such cash flows

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Overdue receivables on account of interest and instalments of standard loans should be slotted as below:

Financing of liquidity gaps:
The negative gap (i.e., where outflows exceed inflows) in the 1 to 14 days and 15 to 28 days time-bucket should not exceed the prudential limit of 10 per cent and 15 per cent respectively of the cash outflows of each time-bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: of 10 per cent and 15 per cent respectively of the cash outflows of each time-bucket

conditions: In case these limits are exceeded
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In case these limits are exceeded, the measures proposed for bringing the gaps within the limit, should be shown by a footnote in the relative statement.
enriched_requirements.object: footnote in the relative statement

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: re-price on the roll- over / re-pricing date should be slotted in respective time buckets as per the re-pricing dates.
enriched_requirements.object: time buckets as per the re-pricing dates

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In the absence of adequate historical database, the entire amount payable under the embedded options should be slotted as per the residual period to the earliest exercise date.
enriched_requirements.object: the residual period to the earliest exercise date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: To be slotted in respective time buckets as per residual maturity or as per residual lock-in period, as the case may be.
The prematurely withdrawable deposits with no lock-in period or past such lock-in period, should be slotted in the earliest /shortest time bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: earliest /shortest time bucket

conditions: In case of borrowings from RBI linked to the Policy Rate
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In case of borrowings from RBI linked to the Policy Rate, the entire amount of borrowing should be slotted in the 1 to 28 days time-bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: 1 to 28 days time-bucket

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: e) Provisions The amount of interest overdue (including the amount pre- funded in the account with RBI for servicing of outstanding old SLR bonds pending claims from investors) should be shown in 1 - 28 days time-bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: - 28 days time-bucket

conditions: In case of floating rate bonds/deposits
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
f) Interest payable on bonds / deposits / borrowings In case of floating rate bonds/deposits, the amount of interest
<image>
outflow may be calculated at the floating rate applicable as on the reporting date.
enriched_requirements.object: floating rate applicable as on the reporting date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The contractual cash outflows under the IRS / FRAs / currency swaps should be placed as per the residual period till each of the contractual cash flows.
enriched_requirements.object: the residual period till each of the contractual cash flows

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The cash flows relating to currency swaps may be shown in the IRS statement of the respective currencies.
enriched_requirements.object: IRS statement of the respective currencies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The FIs which maintain separate 'Trading Book' consisting of securities which comply with the stipulations of paragraph 31 of the Directions and duly approved as such by the Board / ALCO, should be slotted as per their residual maturity (or
<image>
repricing maturity in case of floating rate instruments).
enriched_requirements.object: their residual maturity (or <image> repricing maturity in case of floating rate instruments)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: As regards the dividends on preference shares, particularly on cumulative ones, the bucket may be decided on judgemental basis.
enriched_requirements.object: basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, the bonds / debentures valued by applying NPA norms due to non-servicing of interest, should be shown, net of provisions made, in the time buckets prescribed at items B.7(a) and B.7(b) in Annex III.
enriched_requirements.object: provisions made, in the time buckets prescribed at items B

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, the equity holdings in the assisted companies, acquired as part of the overall financing package, should be slotted as per the specific disinvestment plan formulated for such holdings.
enriched_requirements.object: the specific disinvestment plan formulated for such holdings

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In case, a specific disinvestment plan has not been evolved, the equity holdings should be shown as an inflow in the Non-sensitive bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: inflow in the Non-sensitive bucket

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The interest cash flows on the loans will be sensitive on receipt and should be slotted as per the timing of the cash flow.
enriched_requirements.object: the timing of the cash flow

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The amount of term loans should be slotted in time buckets which correspond to the reset date when the rate might be changed in response to the changes in their PLR or market interest rates.
enriched_requirements.object: buckets which correspond to the reset date when the rate might be changed in response to the changes in their PLR or market interes

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The interest amount would be sensitive on receipt and should be slotted as per the timing of the interest payment.
enriched_requirements.object: the timing of the interest payment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The amount of interest should be calculated at the rate applicable on the reporting date.
enriched_requirements.object: rate applicable on the reporting date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: As regards the future income from assets (such as deposits, loans & investments), the cash inflow over the entire life of the asset should be captured and not only till the reporting date.
enriched_requirements.object: only till the reporting date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, for loan assets, the future income should be reckoned only in respect of standard assets and for debt securities, the interest income should be reckoned as inflow only if the interest is serviced regularly.
enriched_requirements.object: respect of standard assets and for debt securities, the interest income should be reckoned as inflow only if the interest is servi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Such future inflows should be slotted in the relative time buckets as per the stipulated repayment schedule under the terms of sanction.
enriched_requirements.object: relative time buckets as per the stipulated repayment schedule under the terms of sanction

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The contractual cash inflows under the IRS / FRAs / currency swaps should be placed as per the residual period till each of the contractual cash flows.
enriched_requirements.object: the residual period till each of the contractual cash flows
